meaning: n.(文学创作的)传统主题,(修辞学上的)惯用语句
examples: James Joyce USES the topos of the wandering Jew in his Ulysses.
If they are found on the same place, it is iso-topos. These are called isotopes.
He, , for his part, also had a certain sense of the topos, when what was in question was the orderof nature.
The Poles seem keener on unifying than the Germans, according to a study by Topos, a research firm.
